primary_method: component owner-cash DCF plus non-overlapping risked unit NAV
weighting_rationale: The primary method maps each legal economic claim once and forces probability, timing, capital, tax, and realization into the component that causes them. Comparable and replacement-cost methods remain cross-checks because they cannot cure missing ownership or contract evidence.
agreed_facts: The 2025 10-K reports approximately 882,000 surface acres and approximately 224,000 total NRA.
Produced-water royalties are separately disclosed from water sales and services.
Bolt is an investment and development relationship still pursuing anchor customers; Phase 2B remains development-stage.
disputed_facts: No tract/county inventory reconciles total NRA to producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant buckets.
No complete public royalty-versus-operated-water volume, margin, and capital bridge exists.
Bolt and desalination lack the complete enforceable milestone, ownership, and remaining-capital record needed for decision-grade option values.
recommendation: research_blocked: complete the named acceptance tests before any human decision or sizing discussion.

operational_milestones: Quarterly royalty production and revenue bridge
Produced-water royalty and operated-water volume/margin/capex bridge
Phase 2B completion and commercial unit economics
valuation_refresh_triggers: new filing or investor-day project disclosure

price_review_thresholds.action: require contract-backed option evidence before increasing value

thesis_break_conditions: Total NRA cannot be reconciled without overlap
Operated-water incremental after-tax return falls below cost of capital
Bolt or desalination fails its next disclosed milestone
